## [03:06 - 06:07]

solution and what is called buying temperature. Now you're going to use your buying temperature thermometer throughout the entire sales process. So it's really key that you pay attention here. So buying temperature, if someone is hot, that means that the problem that they have keeps them up at night, they're in a great deal of pain and they believe beyond a shadow of a doubt that your solution is exactly what they need. Those are your hot leads. Now if somebody is warm, they might have some pain, maybe it's not enough for them to take action or maybe they know about you but they're not familiar with the solution or maybe they know of your solution but they're not quite convinced that that's the solution for them. This is where we're going to spend the majority of our time is working with those warm leads and heating them up until they're hot and ready to go and ready to sign up and convert into clients. Now cold, if someone is cold, we're going to stay away from cold people. We're not even going to waste our time because they either don't have a problem, they're not in pain, they're not looking for a solution and your job as a sales professional is not to convince people, do not waste your time trying to convince people of how badass your offer is.
